acl.description: Defines the access control list permissions for the object, specifying who can read, write, or manage it; defaults to "private".

aws_s3_object_source.description: Specifies the source file path for the object to be uploaded to the S3 bucket; must be a valid local file path.

bucket.description: Specifies the S3 bucket where the object will be stored; must already exist and be correctly configured.

bucket_key_enabled.description: Enables or disables the use of bucket keys for server-side encryption to reduce the cost of encryption operations.

cache_control.description: Defines caching behavior for the object, specifying how long browsers or intermediate caches should store the object before checking for updates.

checksum_algorithm.description: Specifies the checksum algorithm to validate the integrity of the object during upload; valid values are "CRC32", "CRC32C", "SHA1", or "SHA256".

content.description: Specifies the content to be uploaded to the S3 object; can be plain text, a file path, or binary data.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded content to be uploaded to the S3 object. Ensure the data is properly encoded and within the S3 object size limits.

content_disposition.description: Defines the presentation style of the object, such as inline or attachment, to control how the object is displayed or downloaded.

content_encoding.description: Specifies the encoding type used for the object, such as gzip or compress, to indicate how the object data is compressed.

content_language.description: Specifies the language of the content for the S3 object, formatted as a valid RFC 2616 language code.

content_type.description: Specifies the media type of the object, such as 'text/html' or 'application/json'.

etag.description: The entity tag of the object, used for conditional requests and cache validation.

force_destroy.description: Indicates whether the object should be forcibly deleted, bypassing the bucket's lifecycle rules or any retention settings.

key.description: Specifies the name of the object within the S3 bucket; must be unique within the bucket.

kms_key_id.description: Specifies the ID of the KMS key used to encrypt the S3 object; required if server-side encryption is enabled with AWS KMS.

metadata.description: Defines custom metadata key-value pairs to be stored with the S3 object. Keys and values must be strings.

object_lock_legal_hold_status.description: Specifies the legal hold status of the object; must be either "ON" or "OFF".

object_lock_mode.description: Specifies the object lock mode for the S3 object; valid values are "GOVERNANCE" or "COMPLIANCE".

object_lock_retain_until_date.description: Specifies the retention expiration date for the object, applicable only if object locking is enabled. Must be in ISO 8601 format.

override_provider.description: Specifies an alternate provider configuration to use for managing the S3 object.

server_side_encryption.description: Specifies the server-side encryption method to protect the S3 object, such as AES256 or aws:kms.

source_hash.description: Checksum of the source file used to detect changes and trigger updates to the S3 object.

storage_class.description: Specifies the storage class for the S3 object, such as STANDARD, REDUCED_REDUNDANCY, or GLACIER.

tags.description: Defines metadata for the S3 object as key-value pairs; keys and values must be strings.

website_redirect.description: Specifies the URL or object key to redirect requests for this object; must be a valid URL or S3 object key.
